quiz.title: Unit 3 Quiz 4, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Atomic Radius and Periodic Trends
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Atomic Radius Trends (Down a Group, Across a Period), Effective Nuclear Charge, Group Properties, Period Properties, Element Classification

questions.question: Which of the following is an alkaline earth metal?
questions.explanation: Beryllium (Be) is in Group 2, making it an alkaline earth metal. Boron is a metalloid, sodium is an alkali metal, and aluminum is a post-transition metal.

questions.question: The atomic radius generally increases down a group because:
questions.explanation: Atomic radius increases down a group because each successive element has an additional electron shell (principal energy level), making the atom larger despite increasing nuclear charge.

questions.question: Atomic radius generally decreases across a period because:
questions.explanation: Across a period, electrons are added to the same shell while protons increase in the nucleus. The increasing effective nuclear charge pulls electrons closer, decreasing atomic radius.

questions.question: Which element has the largest atomic radius in Period 2?
questions.explanation: Lithium (Li) is at the beginning of Period 2 and has the largest atomic radius. Atomic radius decreases from left to right across a period due to increasing effective nuclear charge.

questions.question: Which element has the smallest atomic radius in Period 3?
questions.explanation: Chlorine (Cl) has the smallest atomic radius among the Period 3 options shown. Atomic radius decreases across a period, with argon typically having the smallest, but it's not listed.

questions.question: Which group contains elements with the largest atomic radii?
questions.explanation: Group 1 (alkali metals) contains elements with the largest atomic radii in each period because they have the lowest effective nuclear charge on their valence electron.

questions.question: The total number of recognized elements in the modern periodic table is:
questions.explanation: The modern periodic table contains 118 confirmed elements, from hydrogen (Z=1) to oganesson (Z=118).

questions.question: Elements in the same group have:
options: The same number of electron shells
Increasing nuclear charge but unrelated properties
questions.explanation: Elements in the same group have the same number of valence electrons and similar valence electron configurations, which gives them similar chemical properties.

questions.question: Elements in the same period have the same:
questions.explanation: Elements in the same period have the same number of occupied electron shells. For example, all Period 3 elements have electrons in their first three shells.

questions.question: Potassium is an example of:
questions.explanation: Potassium (K, Z=19) is in Group 1, making it an alkali metal. Alkali metals are highly reactive and have one valence electron.

questions.question: Calcium is an example of:
questions.explanation: Calcium (Ca, Z=20) is in Group 2, making it an alkaline earth metal. These elements have two valence electrons and are less reactive than alkali metals.

questions.question: Which atom is the smallest?
questions.explanation: Neon (Ne) is at the end of Period 2 and has the smallest atomic radius. Atomic radius decreases from left to right across a period due to increasing effective nuclear charge.

questions.question: Which atom has the largest atomic radius?
questions.explanation: Lithium (Li) is at the beginning of Period 2 and has the largest atomic radius among these options. Atomic radius decreases across the period.

questions.question: The term 'effective nuclear charge' refers to the ________ felt by valence electrons
questions.explanation: Effective nuclear charge (Z_eff) is the net positive charge experienced by valence electrons after accounting for shielding by inner electrons. It determines how strongly valence electrons are attracted to the nucleus.

questions.question: Which of the following increases atomic radius?
questions.explanation: Adding more electron shells (moving down a group) increases atomic radius. Higher nuclear charge decreases radius, and higher electronegativity/ionization energy are associated with smaller radii.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered atomic radius trends and periodic patterns! 📏⚛️
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the reasons why atomic radius changes across periods and down groups.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on effective nuclear charge and its effect on atomic size.
scoreMessages.0: Please review atomic radius trends, group and period patterns, and effective nuclear charge.
